items.title: BERT-style masked language modeling
items.scenario: A corpus of Wikipedia articles with no human labels. During pretraining, 15% of tokens are masked at random. The model must predict the original tokens from bidirectional context. After weeks on billions of tokens, the encoder produces contextual embeddings used for sentiment, NER, and search with fine-tuning on thousands — not millions — of labeled examples.
items.explanation: The pretext label (masked token) is free from the text itself. Downstream tasks reuse the learned representation rather than the fill-in-the-blank objective.

items.title: GPT-style next-token prediction
items.scenario: Raw web text is fed sequentially. At each position the model predicts the next token; the true next token from the same document serves as the pseudo-label. This causal language modeling objective scales to trillions of tokens and underpins chat models that generate coherent paragraphs.
items.explanation: Next-token prediction is self-supervision: the label is the following token already present in the unlabeled corpus.

items.title: SimCLR contrastive pretraining on images
items.scenario: An unlabeled photo dataset. Two random crops/augmentations of the same image form a positive pair; crops from different images are negatives. A vision encoder is trained so positive pairs have similar embeddings and negatives differ. The encoder later powers image classifiers fine-tuned with only hundreds of labels per class.
items.explanation: Contrastive SSL treats instance identity (same image, different augmentations) as the supervisory signal derived from data.

items.title: Rotation prediction on unlabeled photos
items.scenario: Each training image is rotated by 0°, 90°, 180°, or 270°. The model receives the rotated image and must predict the rotation angle. Correct angles are pseudo-labels computed when the rotation is applied — no human tagger needed.
items.explanation: Simple geometric transforms turn unlabeled images into a 4-class classification pretext task whose real payoff is the learned visual features.
